activities_projects.title: Understanding Work
activities_projects.objective: Learn the concept of work in daily life
steps: Write three examples of doing work at home or school
Identify the force applied and distance moved in each case
Note whether work is done or not
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between situations where work is done and not done

activities_projects.title: Measuring Force
steps: Measure the force needed to lift a small weight using a spring balance
Record the force in Newtons
Repeat with different weights

activities_projects.title: Calculating Work
activities_projects.objective: Compute work done using the formula W = F × d
steps: Apply a known force to move an object a certain distance
Record force and distance
Calculate work done using the formula

activities_projects.title: Identifying Types of Energy
steps: Drop a ball from a height and observe motion
Identify when it has potential and kinetic energy
Write observations in notebook

activities_projects.title: Energy Transformation
steps: Stretch a rubber band and release it
Observe how potential energy converts into kinetic energy
Note the process in the notebook

activities_projects.title: Simple Machines and Work
activities_projects.objective: Learn how machines make work easier
steps: Use a simple machine to lift a small weight
Compare effort with and without the machine
Record observations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects machines to reduction of effort in work

activities_projects.title: Work Done in Daily Life
activities_projects.objective: Identify examples of work in real life
steps: Look for 3 activities at home or school involving work
Describe the force applied and distance moved
Note whether work is positive or zero

activities_projects.title: Calculating Energy in Motion
activities_projects.objective: Calculate kinetic energy of moving objects
steps: Push a toy car along a track
Measure mass and speed of the car
Calculate kinetic energy using KE = 1/2 mv^2
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Basic understanding of kinetic energy calculation

activities_projects.title: Potential Energy in Heights
steps: Lift a weight to a certain height
Record its mass and height
Calculate potential energy using PE = mgh
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns how potential energy depends on mass and height

activities_projects.title: Observing Work Without Movement
activities_projects.objective: Understand situations where no work is done
steps: Push against a wall or hold a weight stationary
Observe that no movement occurs
Conclude work done is zero
